ORDER DENYING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL THE DEPOSITION OF INTEL CORPORATION (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF NATIONAL SECURITY SYSTEMS' MOTION TO COMPEL THE DEPOSITION OF INTEL CORPORATION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE SOUGHT TO TAKE THE DEPOSITION OF DEFENDANT ON A DATE, MAY 13, 2014, THAT WAS LESS THAN 30 DAYS PRIOR TO THE TRIAL DATE OF JUNE 2, 2014. UNDER THE CODE, A DEPOSITION IS DEEMED COMPLETED ON THE DAY IT BEGINS, SEE CCP 2024.010, AND THE CODE REQUIRES THAT DISCOVERY PROCEEDINGS BE COMPLETED 30 DAYS PRIOR TO THE DATE INITIALLY SET FOR TRIAL, SEE CCP 2024.020(A). THOUGH A COURT MAY GRANT LEAVE FOR A PARTY TO COMPLETE DISCOVERY PROCEEDINGS CLOSER TO THE INITIAL TRIAL DATE OR TO REOPEN DISCOVERY AFTER A NEW TRIAL DATE HAS BEEN SET, SEE CCP 2024.050(A), THE JUDGE PRO TEM DOES NOT CONSTRUE THE COURT'S ORDER OF MARCH 4, 2014 AS GRANTING PLAINTIFF LEAVE TO TAKE DEPOSITIONS LESS THAN 30 DAYS BEFORE THE RESET TRIAL DATE OF JUNE 2, 2014. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: ANTHONY SCHOENBERG, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED.=(302/JPT)

Off Calendar Jun-04-2014 Continued To Jun-04-14 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 206

Plaintiff National Security Syestems Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Intel Corporation
Off Calendar May-23-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Charles Samel, Esq. of Perkins Coie LLP (310-788-3214)for Intel Corporation, Defendant and opposing party.
No other appearance on the matter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Plaintiff National Security Systems' Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Intel Corporation is DENIED. Plaintiff's notice sought to take the deposition of Defendant on a date, May 13, 2014, that was less than 30 days prior to the trial date of June 2, 2014. Under the Code, a deposition is deemed completed on the day it begins, see CCP 2024.010, and the Code requires that discovery proceedings be completed 30 days prior to the date initially set for trial, see CCP 2024.020(a). Though a court may grant leave for a party to complete discovery proceedings closer to the initial trial date or to reopen discovery after a new trial date has been set, see CCP 2024.050(a), the Judge Pro Tem does not construe the Court's order of March 4, 2014 as granting Plaintiff leave to take depositions less than 30 days before the reset trial date of June 2, 2014. Order is signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem: Anthony Schoenberg, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported.=(302/JPT)
